purpose: Choose database access and object-relational mapping patterns for enterprise applications.
ask_if_missing: Does the object model closely match the relational schema?
Do business transactions modify many related objects across requests?
assume_if_low_risk: separate SQL into gateways/mappers even for simple systems
buy/use existing O/R mapping tooling for rich Domain Model unless there is a strong reason not to

branches.criteria: Transaction Script and simple SQL access
branches.recommendation: Use Table Data Gateway or Row Data Gateway to keep SQL out of scripts.

branches.criteria: Table Module or Record Set-centric tooling
branches.recommendation: Use Table Data Gateway and let modules operate on record-set data.
Use Active Record if coupling to the schema is acceptable.

branches.criteria: Rich Domain Model, legacy schema, or independent model/schema evolution
branches.recommendation: Use Data Mapper with Unit of Work, Identity Map, and selective Lazy Load; consider O/R mapping tooling.

branches.criteria: Many query shapes or multiple data sources behind a rich domain
branches.recommendation: Add Repository and Query Object over Data Mapper/Metadata Mapping.

failure_modes: SQL is scattered through presentation/domain code
Active Record binds a rich domain model to a relational schema
Data Mapper is hand-built beyond team capacity
Lazy Load causes N+1/ripple-loading performance collapse
missing Identity Map creates two objects for one database row
